LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, MAY-19-2020 HEARING HELD RE: MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL FOR PARTIES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STAY IS GRANTED. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1281.2(D)(3) AND CONCLUDES THAT THE ARBITRATION BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT SHOULD PROCEED FIRST. THE COURT THEREFORE STAYS THIS ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2020 Continued To Jan-13-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration (Stipulated Continuance)
Off Calendar May-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. Gregory J. Korbel, Esq. of Miller, Morton, Caillat & Nevis, LLP (Tel: 408-292-1765) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiff Stacey Frost.
ATTY. Emmett E. Seltzer, Esq. of Kahana & Feld (Tel: 510-859-8017) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Stroub Construction Company, Inc.

HEARING RE: MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION.

MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL FOR PARTIES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

Plaintiff's motion to stay is granted. (See Code Civ. Proc. 1281.4 [stay pending arbitration]; 128(a)(8) [inherent powers of court].) Defendant argues that the statutes that Plaintiff cites apply only when a petition to compel arbitration is pending and/or arbitration is ordered. (See, e.g., Code Civ. Proc. 1281.4, 1281.2.) However, even where no order or application for an order compelling arbitration has been made, "it is within the implicit power of the court to stay the legal proceedings while arbitration is proceeding." (Seidman & Seidman v. Wolfson (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 826, 834.) Here, "although no order or application for order have been made, it would be a useless act to require [plaintiff] to submit such an application . . . ." (Id.) The parties have already briefed the germane issues and defendant does not contend that the adduced arbitration clause does not apply or is unenforceable.

The Korbel declaration does not properly authenticate the alleged construction agreement. Nevertheless, the normal rules of authentication are inapplicable to petitions to compel arbitration and the reply Frost declaration authenticates the agreement. (See Condee v. Longwood (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 215, 218-219.) In addition, defendant does not dispute that the contract that plaintiff proffers, which includes a binding arbitration clause, is the agreement that governs the parties' relationship.

Defendant argues that the court should deny a stay under Code of Civil Procedure 1281.2 because defendant filed claims against subcontractors that are not subject to arbitration. The court exercises its discretion under Code of Civil Procedure 1281.2(d)(3) and concludes that the arbitration between plaintiff and defendant should proceed first. The court therefore stays this action pending arbitration. (See Seidman & Seidman, 50 Cal.App.3d at 834 [stay appropriate where "a multiplicity of actions might be avoided by staying the legal proceedings . . . while the arbitration continues"]; see also, e.g., Heritage Provider Network, Inc. v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1146, 1153 [even "a single overlapping issue is sufficient to require imposition of a stay"]; Federal Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 1370, 1374-1375 ["The purpose of the statutory stay [under section 1281.4] is to protect the jurisdiction of the arbitrator by preserving the status quo until arbitration is resolved. In the absence of a stay, the continuation of the proceedings in the trial court disrupts the arbitration proceedings and can render them ineffective."].)

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
